description: Cross-framework mapping from EU Taxonomy environmental objectives to ESRS (European Sustainability Reporting Standards) disclosure requirements
notes: Mapping between the 6 EU Taxonomy environmental objectives and corresponding ESRS topical standards. Companies reporting under CSRD must disclose Taxonomy KPIs (turnover, CapEx, OpEx) as part of their ESRS reporting. ESRS 2 Appendix C explicitly references Taxonomy disclosure requirements. This mapping identifies which ESRS disclosures provide the underlying data for Taxonomy assessments.

mappings.notes: ESRS E1 covers all climate-related disclosures including GHG emissions, energy, transition plans. Taxonomy Objective 1 substantial contribution criteria directly correspond to ESRS E1 metrics.

mappings.source_name: Climate change mitigation — GHG emissions thresholds
mappings.target_name: Gross Scopes 1, 2, 3 and total GHG emissions
mappings.notes: Taxonomy technical screening criteria often set GHG emission thresholds (e.g., < 100 gCO2e/kWh for electricity generation). ESRS E1-6 requires disclosure of Scope 1, 2, 3 emissions data that feeds into Taxonomy alignment assessment.

Taxonomy criteria for buildings, manufacturing and other activities reference energy consumption and renewable energy share. ESRS E1-5 requires disclosure of total energy consumption, renewable vs non-renewable breakdown, and energy intensity.

mappings.target_name: Transition plan for climate change mitigation
mappings.notes: ESRS E1-1 requires disclosure of transition plan aligned with limiting global warming to 1.5°C. Taxonomy alignment of CapEx plans directly relates to the transition plan disclosures under ESRS.

mappings.target_name: Targets related to climate change mitigation and adaptation
mappings.notes: ESRS E1-4 requires GHG emission reduction targets. Taxonomy alignment assessment considers whether activities support Paris-aligned decarbonisation.
Taxonomy Objective 2 adaptation criteria overlap with ESRS E1 requirements on climate risk assessment, physical risks, and adaptation measures.

mappings.target_name: Anticipated financial effects from material physical and transition risks
mappings.notes: Taxonomy DNSH Appendix A requires climate risk and vulnerability assessment. ESRS E1-9 requires disclosure of anticipated financial effects from physical and transition risks. Both require analysis of acute and chronic physical risks.

mappings.source_name: Sustainable use and protection of water and marine resources
mappings.notes: Taxonomy Objective 3 criteria align with ESRS E3 disclosures on water consumption, water management policies, and impact on water bodies.
Taxonomy water criteria reference Water Framework Directive good status requirements. ESRS E3-4 requires disclosure of total water consumption and water consumption in water-stressed areas.

mappings.target_name: Policies related to water and marine resources
mappings.notes: ESRS E3-1 requires disclosure of policies to manage water impacts, directly relevant to Taxonomy water DNSH criteria.
Taxonomy Objective 4 criteria on waste prevention, reuse and recycling align with ESRS E5 disclosures.
Taxonomy construction criteria require minimum recycled content and reuse. ESRS E5-4 requires disclosure of resource inflows including recycled/secondary materials.

Taxonomy DNSH criteria for circular economy reference waste hierarchy and recycling rates (e.g., 70% CDW recycling for construction). ESRS E5-5 requires disclosure of waste generated and waste treatment including recycling rates.
Taxonomy Objective 5 criteria on pollution prevention align with ESRS E2 disclosures on pollutant emissions to air, water, and soil.

mappings.target_name: Pollution of air, water and soil
mappings.notes: Taxonomy DNSH pollution criteria reference BAT-AEL limits under Industrial Emissions Directive. ESRS E2-4 requires disclosure of pollutant emissions quantities.

mappings.target_name: Substances of concern and substances of very high concern
mappings.notes: Taxonomy DNSH criteria require building materials not to contain substances of very high concern (SVHC). ESRS E2-5 requires disclosure of substances of concern produced or used.

mappings.source_name: Protection and restoration of biodiversity and ecosystems
mappings.notes: Taxonomy Objective 6 criteria on biodiversity conservation align with ESRS E4 disclosures.

mappings.target_name: Impact metrics related to biodiversity and ecosystems change
mappings.notes: Taxonomy DNSH Appendix D requires EIA for biodiversity-sensitive areas. ESRS E4-5 requires disclosure of impact metrics including sites near protected areas.

mappings.target_name: Anticipated financial effects from material physical and transition risks
mappings.notes: Taxonomy Appendix A DNSH requires climate risk assessment considering acute and chronic physical risks. ESRS E1-9 requires the same climate risk analysis.

Taxonomy minimum safeguards reference OECD Guidelines, UNGPs, ILO standards. ESRS S1 covers own workforce topics including due diligence. Partial because safeguards also cover supply chain (ESRS S2) and communities (ESRS S3).

mappings.source_name: Minimum social safeguards — Human rights due diligence
mappings.notes: Taxonomy safeguards require human rights due diligence per UNGPs. ESRS S1-1 requires disclosure of policies related to own workforce including human rights commitments.

mappings.target_name: Prevention and detection of corruption and bribery
mappings.notes: Taxonomy minimum safeguards cover anti-corruption (referencing OECD Guidelines Chapter VII). ESRS G1-3 requires disclosure of anti-corruption policies and prevention measures.

mappings.target_name: Disclosures in relation to specific circumstances
mappings.notes: ESRS 2 Appendix C requires undertakings to disclose Taxonomy-related information. The Taxonomy Disclosures Delegated Act specifies the KPI templates. ESRS integrates but does not duplicate Taxonomy reporting requirements.
